what_it_does: This file is the HTML entry point for a single-page canvas application: it declares the document shell, loads Google Fonts, sets PWA and viewport metadata, defines the favicon/touch-icon and theme color, and mounts the React/TypeScript root via a script tag pointing to /src/main.tsx.
observations: The viewport meta disables user scaling (maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no) and includes a comment explaining it prevents accidental page-zoom on touch devices while tldraw canvas zoom remains unaffected.
It declares full-screen PWA capabilities for iOS and Android (apple-mobile-web-app-capable, mobile-web-app-capable).
The title is 'the company · canvas' while the home-screen short title is 'Vi'.
Theme color is #d4af37 (gold) and both favicon and apple-touch-icon point to /vi-classic-gold.png.
It loads Fraunces and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts with preconnect hints.
The body contains only a root div and a module script to /src/main.tsx.
standouts: The unusually explicit comment justifying the non-scalable viewport as a deliberate app-canvas UX choice.
PWA full-screen meta tags paired with viewport-fit=cover for an immersive app shell.
Distinctive font pairing (Fraunces serif + IBM Plex Mono) and a consistent gold brand asset.
conventions: Follows Vite-style convention of a module script pointing to /src/main.tsx and a <div id="root"> mount point.
Uses common PWA meta tags and Google Fonts preconnect/link pattern.
Breaks the typical accessibility guideline against user-scalable=no, but documents the intentional trade-off.
concerns: Disabling user scaling can reduce accessibility for users who need pinch-to-zoom.
No <noscript> fallback for users without JavaScript.
Relies on external Google Fonts and an external icon asset.
No Content-Security-Policy, meta description, or Open Graph tags.
viewport-fit=cover can cause content to overlap notches/safe areas if CSS safe-area insets are not handled.
notes: A branded, PWA-oriented HTML shell for a canvas app that intentionally locks browser zoom to keep the drawing surface predictable.
questions: Is the app built with React, or is main.tsx just a TypeScript entry point?
What do 'the company' and 'Vi' refer to in the product/branding context?
Is there an associated service worker or manifest for the PWA claims?
Was the accessibility trade-off of disabling zoom reviewed or tested with assistive-tech users?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the title and metadata frame it as an immersive canvas app shell, and the file behaves as the HTML entry point that loads the application and configures app-like viewport/PWA behavior.
apparent_themes: Immersive app-like canvas experience over traditional web page behavior.
Strong typographic and color brand identity.
Documented intentional trade-offs rather than defaulting to browser norms.
intention_signals: It appears designed to launch a full-screen, touch-friendly drawing/canvas application (likely tldraw-based) under a 'Vi'/'the company' brand, using a serif+mono typographic identity and gold accent, while preventing browser zoom from disrupting the canvas interaction.
novelty: The viewport comment explicitly ties the zoom-lock decision to a real user finding ('Tim's phone finding') and notes that tldraw's own canvas zoom remains unaffected, making the rationale file-local and concrete.
contribution: Provides the document shell, PWA and viewport metadata, external font loading, brand icon/theme configuration, and the DOM mount point for the TypeScript application entry.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML5 app shell for a canvas PWA named 'the company · canvas' / 'Vi' that loads Fraunces and IBM Plex Mono fonts, sets a gold theme and touch icon, locks browser zoom for canvas usability, and mounts /src/main.tsx into a root div.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the branded, full-screen, mobile-first entry surface for a tldraw-style canvas application while preventing accidental browser zoom.
